key_insight: A Lagrangian trajectory-based method reconstructs polymeric stress in viscoelastic flows from deformation history, avoiding full-domain Eulerian constitutive solves.
engineering_relevance: This enables stress-field estimation directly from measured velocity data, making viscoelastic flow analysis more practical for experiments and complex geometries.
method: Integrate deformation-gradient history along fluid-element paths in a known steady velocity field.
Reconstruct polymeric stress for FENE-P and Oldroyd-B models without solving the full transport equation.
Apply the scheme to canonical flows and obstacle-laden channel flows from simulations and microfluidic experiments.
evidence_signal: The reconstructed stress fields agree with traditional Eulerian reference solutions in both numerical simulations and experimental velocity-field data.
watch_item: The approach assumes a known, steady velocity field, so performance may be limited for strongly unsteady or poorly resolved measurements.
paper_title: Lagrangian evaluation of polymeric stress in viscoelastic fluids
